programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: Slow TV is back, celebrating stunning landscapes of Countries across Australia. Sit back and relax with the healing powers of Country.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: Slow TV is back, celebrating stunning landscapes of Countries across Australia. Sit back and relax with the healing powers of Country.

programme.title: Toi Time
programme.desc: Tama sells kai at the market, Buzz shows Tama how to make burgers and Aunty Anika explains good manners at the dinner table.

programme.title: Waabiny Time
programme.desc: Djinang, look! It's a yongka, a kangaroo. And can you see the wetj, the emu full of feathers?

programme.title: Bird's Eye View
programme.desc: The baby humans have erupted into song; this must mean they're hungry! Luckily Kereru is here to save the day with a big juicy mouthful of...wriggly worms.

programme.title: Aussie Bush Tales
programme.desc: Three mischievous Aboriginal boys and their cousin, Jedda, always followed by their dingo puppy, Snowy, go exploring and investigate new and exciting mysteries in the Aussie bush.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities of Gold
programme.desc: After Ambrosius sets them free, our heroes learn that the fresco is a copy of The Eyes That See All, located in Tibet.

programme.title: Bushwhacked!
programme.desc: Brandon takes Kayne to the Great Barrier Reef to track down one of the greatest sights in the animals kingdom: baby turtles racing for the sea minutes after they are born.

programme.title: Motown Magic
programme.sub-title: Money, That's What I Want And Master Blaster
programme.desc: When Ella uses the Love family's vacation fund to buy a new toy, Ben turns to the Street Art World to help Ella pay back the money.

programme.title: The Magic Canoe
programme.desc: Julie meets Passifou, the little gannets' fool. She would like to keep him forever, but the baby gets bored and ends up running away.

programme.title: Spartakus and the Sun Beneath the Sea
programme.desc: In the city of Arkadia, the children note that life without Shagshag becomes more and more difficult.

programme.title: The World According to Grandpa
programme.desc: Stanley asks 'How do aeroplanes fly?' and Grandpa spins one of his tall tales about a young boy named Cooper who sets about making the first ever flying machine.

programme.title: Chums
programme.desc: The chums find out that the squirrels have taken over the pow wow grounds for their own games. Can they find a way to share the land?

programme.title: Waterworld Africa
programme.desc: Source to Sea is the story of a river and its lifetime endeavor to deliver water from mountain to ocean, completing the Earth's stunningly well designed hydrological system.

programme.title: NITV News Update
programme.desc: NITV News recaptures the highlights of Indigenous news around the country.

programme.title: Karla Grant Presents
programme.desc: The story of a group of men who knocked down the barriers of racism on the sporting field in Darwin.

programme.title: Pro Bull Riding - USA
programme.desc: High-stakes bull riding. Rivalries ignite, champions rise, and every ride pushes the limits in a thrilling new season of action, danger, and determination. Tune in for pure adrenaline.

programme.title: Over the Black Dot
programme.desc: In 2026, Over the Black Dot becomes a digital-first Rugby League yarn free-flowing conversations on footy, culture and community, with panellists and guests sharing authentic Indigenous stories.

programme.title: Inside the Huddle
programme.desc: What is it like to be a footballer? Candid conversations, laughs, and insights that go beyond the game and into the outer.

programme.title: Island Echoes with Nornie Bero
programme.sub-title: Memories and Flavours of Thursday Island
programme.desc: Nornie returns to Thursday Island, a place close to her heart and where she spent much of her childhood. She reconnects with old friends, then reminisces about life on the island and its rich cultural heritage.

programme.title: The Cook Up with Adam Liaw
programme.desc: ARIA award-winning singer Montaigne and executive chef Adam Wolfers are in the kitchen with Adam exploring all the possibilities when cooking with chilli and garlic.

programme.title: Spartakus and the Sun Beneath the Sea
programme.desc: In the mountains, our heroes discover the entrance to a temple. They are greeted by a large priest wearing a mask with the head of a bird.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities of Gold
programme.desc: According to the Ambrosius' plan, Laguerra and the soldiers find a deserted village. Laguerra spots the children hidden in the trees. She hesitates, but leaves without saying anything to her men.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities of Gold
programme.desc: While in the village, the children try to repair the Condor. Pichu, sent by Tao, finds them and delivers a message from Atanaos. He reveals that the Raja was drugged by Ambrosius.

programme.title: Little J & Big Cuz
programme.desc: Little J and Big Cuz discover that super heroes come in all sizes.

programme.title: Motown Magic
programme.sub-title: I'm A Roadrunner And Smiling Faces Sometimes
programme.desc: Ben and Ella are forced to become roommates when Uncle Rod moves in. But it's not long before they get on each other's nerves and Ben declares war! When Ben can't seem to win, he visits Street Art World where a conflict between Jimmy Mack and Dancing Machine helps Ben understand the futility of war.

programme.title: Harlem Globetrotters: Play It Forward
programme.desc: Tryouts for new Globetrotter Team Members are underway! Who will make the cut? Meanwhile, Jet, Wham, and Hotshot teach some South Central LA youth about the benefits of sports and exercise.

programme.title: Garma: Celebrating 25 Years
programme.desc: Stories from Yolngu Country celebrating 25 years of Garma.

programme.title: Te Ao with Moana
programme.desc: A weekly current affairs show that examines New Zealand and international stories through a Maori lens.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: Slow TV is back with more beautiful Bamay. Bamay III celebrates great Australian islands and saltwater country. Sit back and relax with the healing powers of Country.

programme.title: NITV News Update
programme.desc: NITV News recaptures the highlights of Indigenous news around the country.

programme.title: Waterworld Africa
programme.desc: The wetland landscapes of southern Africa are home to a rich tapestry of life.This is usually a land of plenty but for the last 20 years, the St Lucia area has received below average rainfall.

programme.title: NITV Weather
programme.desc: Australian First Nations essential weekend weather forecasts, preparedness tips and cultural knowledge.

programme.title: Going Places With Ernie Dingo
programme.desc: Mark takes in the dramatic sea cliffs of the Tasman Peninsula by boat and learns the art of whisky distilling. Ernie visits a safe haven for the adorable but endangered Tasmanian Devil.

programme.title: Bones Of Crows
programme.desc: Taylor and Aline travel to the Vatican, where Aline confronts her past and inspires a pivotal discovery. Through art and music, the entire Spears family comes together.

programme.title: Django Unchained
programme.desc: With the help of his mentor, a slave-turned-bounty hunter sets out to rescue his wife from a brutal Mississippi plantation owner.

programme.title: Who We Are: Brave New Clan
programme.desc: Follow the lives of six exceptional young Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islanders, and see how they engage with their communities, history and cultures, in modern Australia.

programme.title: Going Places With Ernie Dingo
programme.desc: Ernie travels to Kakadu, takes a boat cruise up East Alligator River, meets photographer Louise, spends time with a ranger, and takes a helicopter with Steve - who was originally a policeman.

programme.title: NITV on the Road: Yabun
programme.desc: From our travelling music series, we showcase veterans and newcomers alike as they perform up on the Yabun stage at Victoria Park, Sydney.

programme.title: Nitv On The Road: Barunga Festival
programme.desc: From our travelling music series, we showcase veterans and newcomers alike as they perform at the Barunga Festival 2015.

programme.title: NITV On the Road: Mbantua
programme.desc: A weekend of culture and music in central Australia.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: Slow TV is back, celebrating stunning landscapes of Countries across Australia. Sit back and relax with the healing powers of Country.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: Slow TV is back, celebrating stunning landscapes of Countries across Australia. Sit back and relax with the healing powers of Country.

programme.title: Toi Time
programme.desc: Jojo makes a Taraka Hangarua and Buzz visits a Transfer Station to see where the recycling goes. Tama, Maia and Jojo recycle their cans, bottles, jars and lids.

programme.title: Waabiny Time
programme.desc: In Noongar Boodgar, Noongar Country there's so much to see. Wano, this way the djet, the flowers and ali bidi, that way you can see the boorn, the trees. Moorditj!

programme.title: Bird's Eye View
programme.desc: The birds are puzzled as to where the adult humans go every day. Why do they do this and could they perhaps be smarter than they look?

programme.title: Aussie Bush Tales
programme.desc: Boya is very happy when he finds a possible new pet, but this pet is VERY noisy.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities of Gold
programme.desc: At the location of The Eyes That See All, the Yellow Dragon ignited a path of fire to the top of a Himalayan mountain. Back on the ship, Ambrosius and the children notice somebody has come onboard.

programme.title: Bushwhacked!
programme.desc: Kayne's challenge? To race the biggest fish in the world, the whale shark, at the stunning Ningaloo Reef in WA. Problem is, they're a little harder to find than first expected.

programme.title: Motown Magic
programme.sub-title: I Cant Help Myself Sugar Pie Honey Bunch And Don't You Worry Bout A Thing
programme.desc: Imaginative boy Ben transforms his city by bringing colourful street art to life, armed with a magic paintbrush and the classic sounds of motown.

programme.title: The Magic Canoe
programme.desc: Pam doesn't say what she really wants and accumulates frustrations. When she meets the chicoque (skunk in the Cree and Metis language), she realises that it would be better to say what bothers her.

programme.title: Spartakus and the Sun Beneath the Sea
programme.desc: Without suspecting that they are being watched and followed by pirates, our heroes venture into a jungle which shelters strange remains.

programme.title: The World According to Grandpa
programme.desc: Louie asks 'Who invented dogs?' and Grandpa spins one of his tall tales about a young girl called Megan who discovers a Teapot Genie in the attic of her new house!

programme.title: Chums
programme.desc: Blueberries are missing and Pterry blames his chum Romper. Can the chums find out who is taking the Blueberries?

programme.title: Waterworld Africa
programme.desc: The wetland landscapes of southern Africa are home to a rich tapestry of life.This is usually a land of plenty but for the last 20 years, the St Lucia area has received below average rainfall.

programme.title: NITV News Update
programme.desc: NITV News recaptures the highlights of Indigenous news around the country.

programme.title: Going Places With Ernie Dingo
programme.desc: Mark takes in the dramatic sea cliffs of the Tasman Peninsula by boat and learns the art of whisky distilling. Ernie visits a safe haven for the adorable but endangered Tasmanian Devil.

programme.title: Coextinction
programme.desc: After a mother orca carries her dead calf for 17 days, two filmmakers spring into action, joining Indigenous leaders and scientists making a final attempt to save the endangered orca.

programme.title: Anthem Sessions
programme.sub-title: Dan Sultan, Bart Willoughby and Rochelle Pitt Watson
programme.desc: This program showcases iconic songs for this land's First Peoples from some of our very best song men and women.

programme.title: Island Echoes with Nornie Bero
programme.desc: Nornie journeys to Hammond Island to explore its unique blend of cultures and rich history.

programme.title: The Cook Up with Adam Liaw
programme.desc: Adam, Christine Manfield and Helly Raichura are making a fantasy a reality as they get food on the table in 30 minutes.

programme.title: Spartakus and the Sun Beneath the Sea
programme.desc: In the jungle, our heroes accompany Ma-Toot, who is looking for her son, Thot. Meanwhile, not far from there, pirates are working to restore an old park of attractions.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities of Gold
programme.desc: The fort is taken by assault from all sides, and the villagers, Tao and Atanaos are freed. Esteban manages to inject the antidote into the Raja, who regains his spirits and realises the betrayal of Ambrosius.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities of Gold
programme.desc: With his father Athanaos finally found, he and Esteban spend time together. It's revealed that Ambrosius was his father's friend when they were young.

programme.title: Little J & Big Cuz
programme.desc: Big Cuz fears she's bitten off more than she can chew when she offers to organise the school's radio broadcast.

programme.title: Motown Magic
programme.desc: Imaginative boy Ben transforms his city by bringing colourful street art to life, armed with a magic paintbrush and the classic sounds of motown.

programme.title: Harlem Globetrotters: Play It Forward
programme.desc: Hotshot and X-Over teach basketball skills to kids with dwarfism at the \.

programme.title: Garma: Celebrating 25 Years
programme.desc: Stories from Yolngu Country celebrating 25 years of Garma.

programme.title: NITV News: Nula
programme.desc: The latest news from the oldest living culture, join Natalie Ahmat and the team of journalists for stories from an Indigenous perspective.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.sub-title: Murrumbidgee River - Madi Madi, Dadi Dadi & Nganguruku Country
programme.desc: A slow TV showcase of the stunning landscapes found in Madi Madi, Dadi Dadi and Nganguruku Country along the waters of the Murrumbidgee River.

programme.title: The Big Dry
programme.desc: The Luangwa Valley in Zambia experiences one of the toughest dry seasons imaginable. Seven months with no rain and spiraling temperatures.

programme.title: NITV Weather
programme.desc: Australian First Nations essential weekend weather forecasts, preparedness tips and cultural knowledge.

programme.title: The Boy and the Heron
programme.desc: A grieving boy enters a mysterious tower, meeting a trickster heron who guides him through fantastical worlds. As reality blurs, he confronts loss, courage, and the fragile balance holding both realms together.

programme.title: First Nation Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Ivy and Steven meet up with Uncle Koodah to learn how to make a \.

programme.title: Jumpin' Jack Flash
programme.desc: An American computer whiz who works at a New York bank is suddenly thrust into the world of international espionage after she inadvertently receives a coded message from a British spy.

programme.title: NITV News: Nula
programme.desc: The latest news from the oldest living culture, join Natalie Ahmat and the team of journalists for stories from an Indigenous perspective.

programme.title: Prison Songs
programme.desc: The inmates of a Darwin prison are shown in a completely new light in Australia's first ever documentary musical Prison Songs, as they share their feelings, faults and experiences in an extraordinary way - through song. Darwin Correctional Centre is the largest correctional centre in the Northern Territory. Originally designed to hold 115 prisoners when it opened in 1979, by 2014 it is overcrowded and bursting with more than 800 male and female inmates. 80% of the prisoners here are Indigenous Australians. Granted unprecedented access to film inside Berrimah before its decommission in late 2014, Prison Songs offers a rare glimpse into life in a contemporary Australian prison. (Commissioned by SBS) (Documentary) M(L) CC.

programme.title: Going Places With Ernie Dingo
programme.desc: Ernie visits the breathtaking Cradle Mountain in Tasmania and meets a dedicated pilot, a focused young ranger, a wildlife conservationist, and an insightful young Palawa man.

programme.title: NITV on the Road: Yabun
programme.desc: From our travelling music series, we showcase veterans and newcomers alike as they perform up on the Yabun stage at Victoria Park, Sydney.

programme.title: Nitv On The Road: Barunga Festival
programme.desc: From our travelling music series, we showcase veterans and newcomers alike as they perform at the Barunga Festival 2015.

programme.title: NITV On the Road: Mbantua
programme.desc: A weekend of culture and music in central Australia.
